strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of Washington (UW) is a premier public research university located in Seattle, known for its top-tier STEM programs, particularly in Computer Science and Engineering. It employs a holistic admissions process that heavily favors in-state residents, while admission to 'capacity-constrained' majors is exceptionally competitive for all applicants.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Out-of-state acceptance into the Allen School of Computer Science & Engineering is historically around 2%.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Non-resident applicants aiming for CS must consider UW a 'reach' school and should utilize alternate major strategies carefully, as internal transfers into CS are nearly impossible.

analyst_takeaways.insight: UW utilizes a 'capacity-constrained' major system where being admitted to the university does not guarantee admission to popular majors.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants must apply 'Direct to Major' or 'Direct to College' for Engineering, CS, and Business to avoid getting stuck in a pre-major with no clear path to their desired degree.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The Husky Promise guarantees full tuition coverage for eligible lower-income in-state students.
analyst_takeaways.implication: UW represents an exceptional ROI for Washington residents, but out-of-state students will face a steep cost of attendance with limited institutional need-based aid.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Large, vibrant urban campus in the University District with a strong Greek life, competitive Pac-12 (moving to Big Ten) athletics, and diverse student organizations.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Highly integrated into Seattle's urban transit network, providing students with easy access to downtown and the broader tech/medical hubs.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: R1 research institution with massive funding, heavily partnered with local giants like Amazon, Microsoft, and Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center for undergraduate research and pipelines.

current_status.test_policy_details: Permanently test-optional/test-blind. Scores are not required for admission and are generally only considered if high (1400+/31+) for applicants who may otherwise not be admitted.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist spots and admits based on preliminary release data.

gpa.notes: UW primarily considers unweighted GPA and course rigor.

testing.policy_note: Less than 20% of enrolled students submitted test scores.

colleges.name: Allen School of Computer Science & Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Only for top-tier STEM students with extensive programming/math backgrounds. Must select as first-choice major.
colleges.student_archetype: Tech-driven innovators targeting Big Tech roles.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Must list as first-choice major on application to be considered for Direct to Major admission.
majors.notes: Internal transfer is exceptionally difficult and space is extremely limited.

colleges.name: College of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students certain they want to study engineering. Placed into a 'pre-major' pool and must place into specific engineering majors later.

majors.name: Engineering (Pre-Major)
majors.notes: Students admitted Direct to College explore engineering disciplines before being placed into a specific major.

application_deadlines.notes: Decisions released March 1-15. UW does not offer Early Decision or Early Action for the Seattle campus.

supplemental_requirements.details: Requires a primary personal statement and a short response regarding community/diversity.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Residency is heavily weighted. In-state students have a significant statistical advantage.

major_selection_tactics: Apply directly to capacity-constrained majors (CS, Engineering) as a first-choice if interested; doing otherwise severely hinders chances.
List a less constrained or 'open' major as a second choice, but ensure genuine interest.
Use the personal statement to explain your backup plan if not admitted to your first-choice major.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: UW considers alternate majors if space allows, but applying to highly selective alternate majors (like CS as a backup) will not work. Choose open or minimum-requirement majors as a secondary option to secure general admission.

financials.tuition_model: Flat rate for full-time enrollment per term/year.

scholarships.name: Purple and Gold Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable over four years for out-of-state students.

scholarships.name: Husky Promise
scholarships.benefits: Guarantees full tuition for eligible lower-income Washington state residents.

ap_policy.general_rule: Scores of 3 or higher are generally considered for college credit or placement.
exceptions: Chemistry and Biology majors may require specific higher scores or recommend taking honors versions despite AP credit.
ap_policy.usage: Used for graduation credit and prerequisite clearing, but cannot waive admission requirements.

ib_policy.general_rule: Scores of 5, 6, or 7 generally receive credit.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Students duplicate subject content with AP/IB and a college course will only be awarded credit for one.

what_it_takes: Maximum course rigor available at your high school
Demonstrated depth in a few extracurriculars rather than a long, shallow list
essay_tips: Address your second-choice major and backup plans in the essay
Focus on explaining how experiences shaped your thinking
Highlight resilience and meaningful community involvement
red_flags: Applying to a capacity-constrained major as a second choice
Relying on legacy status (it is not considered)
insights: UW is essentially two different universities in terms of admissions: one for Washington residents and one for out-of-state students.
The CS program acts as an ultra-elite mini-college within a larger public university.
